message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is building a take-home Spring Boot backend for a pharmacovigilance (PV) case review platform (case ID `PV-2026-0451`), following a multi-phase brief: Phase 1A (Java/Spring Boot backend with merge logic for follow-up ICSR case data) — now complete and verified; Phase 1B (Docker/ops scripts) — in progress; Phase 2 (React frontend) — not started. Within this session, the user's explicit sequential requests were:
   - Fix a validation error message bug: `POST /queries` with a blank `question` field returned `"question question is required"` (field name duplicated).
   - Configure Jackson globally to omit `null` fields from all JSON responses (specifically `status`/`previous_value` showing as `null` on the baseline case).
   - Write the backend `README.md`: must include exactly 4 curl examples (one per "main" endpoint) using the real case ID `PV-2026-0451`, plus a section explaining merge behavior and the `RETAINED` status design decision — "Keep it practical and easy like something that actually easily readable."
   - Discuss (don't yet implement) how to handle two architectural gaps needed for upcoming `backup.sh`/`restore.sh` scripts: (a) no way to list all case IDs, (b) no way to restore a case's exact prior state without triggering merge logic. Then, after agreeing on the design, explicitly: "Go ahead and add both. And yes, make it clear in the code comments that PUT /cases/{caseId} is ops-only — not a normal update path."
   - Start Phase 1B: write the Dockerfile first. Explicit requirements: multi-stage build (one stage to build the jar, one to run it), JRE (not JDK) in the runtime stage, non-root user in the runtime stage, pinned base image versions (not `:latest`), a `HEALTHCHECK` hitting `/health`. Explicitly asked: "What base images would you pick for Java 17?"
   - Before `backup.sh`: write `docker-compose.yml` (the brief asks for it separately) — just the backend service, include a healthcheck. Explicitly asked: "What should restart policy be for a local dev setup?"
   - Most recent request: "Now let's do ops/run.sh first before backup and restore. The brief needs subcommands: build, start, stop, test, logs, clean, and --help. Should fail gracefully if Docker isn't running."

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Spring Boot 3.3.4, Java 17, Gradle wrapper, Lombok (`@Data`/`@Builder`)
   - In-memory `ConcurrentHashMap`-backed `CaseStore` (no database/persistence — critical to several design decisions)
   - Merge semantics: `MergeStatus` enum (`NEW`, `OVERRIDDEN`, `UNCHANGED`, `RETAINED`) computed once during merge and persisted as part of the stored `Case`/`FieldRecord`, not derived live on read
   - Global Jackson config: `spring.jackson.property-naming-strategy: SNAKE_CASE` and `spring.jackson.default-property-inclusion: non_null` in `application.yml`
   - Bean validation (`jakarta.validation.constraints.NotBlank`) + `@RestControllerAdvice`-based `GlobalExceptionHandler`
   - REST semantics distinction: `POST /follow-ups` = business merge operation; `PUT /cases/{caseId}` = ops-only raw overwrite/upsert (no merge)
   - Docker multi-stage builds, Eclipse Temurin base images, `arm64`/`amd64` multi-platform manifest availability differences (Alpine vs Debian/Ubuntu-based images), `HEALTHCHECK` instruction, non-root container users (`groupadd`/`useradd` vs Alpine's `addgroup -S`/`adduser -S`)
   - Docker Compose `restart` policies (`no`, `always`, `on-failure`, `unless-stopped`) and `healthcheck:` block syntax
   - Bash scripting conventions: `set -euo pipefail`, `BASH_SOURCE`-based path resolution, subcommand dispatch via `case`, graceful pre-flight checks (`docker info`) instead of letting raw errors propagate
   - Mockito-based unit testing (`@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`, `@Mock`, `verify`, `verifyNoInteractions`, `thenAnswer`)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`backend/src/main/java/com/pvreview/dto/QueryRequest.java`**
     - Fixed duplicated validation message bug. Changed all three `@NotBlank` messages from `"caseId is required"` / `"fieldPath is required"` / `"question is required"` to just `"is required"`, since `GlobalExceptionHandler.handleValidation` already prepends `error.getField()`.
     - Final relevant fields:
       ```java
       @NotBlank(message = "is required")
       private String caseId;
       @NotBlank(message = "is required")
       private String fieldPath;
       @NotBlank(message = "is required")
       private String question;
       ```

   - **`backend/src/main/resources/application.yml`**
     - Added `default-property-inclusion: non_null` under `spring.jackson` to globally omit null fields from JSON responses.
     - Final relevant section:
       ```yaml
       jackson:
         property-naming-strategy: SNAKE_CASE
         default-property-inclusion: non_null
       ```

   - **`backend/README.md`**
     - Fully rewritten from a placeholder. Contains: stack overview, run/test instructions, bootstrap note (case `PV-2026-0451` loads automatically from `case_v1.json`), 4 curl examples (GET case, POST follow-up merge, POST query, GET queries list) each followed by real verified JSON responses, a merge status table, a "Why RETAINED exists" explanation paragraph, and an error-response reference table. All 4 curl examples were live-verified against the running app and match exactly (IDs/timestamps differ per run but structure/values confirmed correct).

   - **`backend/src/main/java/com/pvreview/repository/CaseStore.java`**
     - Added `findAll()` method:
       ```java
       public List<Case> findAll() {
           return new ArrayList<>(cases.values());
       }
       ```

   - **`backend/src/main/java/com/pvreview/service/CaseService.java`**
     - Added `getAllCases()`:
       ```java
       public List<Case> getAllCases() {
           return caseStore.findAll();
       }
       ```
     - Added `restoreCase()` with ops-only comment and path/body mismatch guard:
       ```java
       // Ops restore only (backup.sh/restore.sh) — writes the snapshot back verbatim, bypassing merge entirely.
       public Case restoreCase(String caseId, Case snapshot) {
           if (snapshot.getCaseId() != null && !Objects.equals(snapshot.getCaseId(), caseId)) {
               throw new IllegalArgumentException(
                       "Path case id " + caseId + " does not match body case id " + snapshot.getCaseId());
           }
           snapshot.setCaseId(caseId);
           return caseStore.save(snapshot);
       }
       ```
     - Added `import java.util.Objects;`

   - **`backend/src/main/java/com/pvreview/controller/CaseController.java`**
     - Added `GET /cases` and `PUT /cases/{caseId}`:
       ```java
       @GetMapping
       public ResponseEntity<List<Case>> getAllCases() {
           return ResponseEntity.ok(caseService.getAllCases());
       }
       ...
       // Ops restore only (backup.sh/restore.sh) — not a normal update path; use POST /{caseId}/follow-ups for that.
       @PutMapping("/{caseId}")
       public ResponseEntity<Case> restoreCase(@PathVariable String caseId, @RequestBody Case snapshot) {
           return ResponseEntity.ok(caseService.restoreCase(caseId, snapshot));
       }
       ```
     - Added imports for `List`, `PutMapping`.

   - **`backend/src/main/java/com/pvreview/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java`**
     - Added `IllegalArgumentException` handler:
       ```java
       @ExceptionHandler(IllegalArgumentException.class)
       public ResponseEntity<ErrorResponse> handleIllegalArgument(IllegalArgumentException ex, WebRequest request) {
           return buildResponse(HttpStatus.BAD_REQUEST, ex.getMessage(), request);
       }
       ```

   - **`backend/src/test/java/com/pvreview/service/CaseServiceTest.java`**
     - Previously written from scratch (12+5 split was actually 5 tests here originally: getCaseReturnsStoredCase, getCaseThrowsWhenNotFound, applyFollowUpThrowsWhenCaseNotFound, applyFollowUpMergesAndSavesResult, bootstrapBuildsBaselineCaseWithNoStatusAndSavesIt).
     - Added a `@Mock MergeService mergeServiceMock` field and `caseServiceWithMockedMerge()` helper alongside the existing real `MergeService mergeService` and `caseService()` helper.
     - Added 4 new tests: `getAllCasesReturnsWhateverTheStoreHas`, `restoreCaseSavesSnapshotVerbatimWithoutTouchingMergeService` (verifies `verifyNoInteractions(mergeServiceMock)`), `restoreCaseSetsCaseIdFromPathWhenBodyOmitsIt`, `restoreCaseThrowsWhenBodyCaseIdConflictsWithPath`.
     - Total test count in this file: 9. Combined with 12 in `MergeServiceTest.java`, total suite ≈ 21 tests, all passing.

   - **`backend/src/test/java/com/pvreview/service/MergeServiceTest.java`** — unchanged in this session (was written in prior session); 12 tests covering all merge statuses, version/classification/missingFields handling.

   - **`backend/Dockerfile`** (new file)
     - Final content:
       ```dockerfile
       # ---- Build stage ----
       FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-jammy AS build
       WORKDIR /app

       COPY gradlew settings.gradle build.gradle ./
       COPY gradle ./gradle
       RUN ./gradlew dependencies --no-daemon

       COPY src ./src
       RUN ./gradlew bootJar --no-daemon

       # ---- Runtime stage ----
       FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy AS runtime
       WORKDIR /app

       RUN groupadd --system pvreview && useradd --system --gid pvreview pvreview
       COPY --from=build /app/build/libs/*.jar app.jar
       RUN chown pvreview:pvreview app.jar
       USER pvreview

       EXPOSE 8080
       HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=20s --retries=3 \
         CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

       ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]
       ```
     - Originally used `eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine` for runtime, but this had no `arm64` manifest (confirmed via `docker pull` failure: "no matching manifest for linux/arm64/v8") — switched to `17-jre-jammy` and changed Alpine-style `addgroup -S`/`adduser -S` to Debian-style `groupadd --system`/`useradd --system`.
     - Verified `wget`/`curl` both present in `eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy` before relying on `wget` for HEALTHCHECK.
     - Build verified: image size 446MB, runs as `uid=999(pvreview)`, `/health` returns `{"status":"UP"}`, Docker HEALTHCHECK reports "healthy", `PV-2026-0451` reachable.

   - **`backend/.dockerignore`** (new file)
     ```
     build/
     .gradle/
     out/
     *.log
     .idea/
     *.iml
     ```

   - **`backend/docker-compose.yml`** (new file)
     - Final content:
       ```yaml
       services:
         backend:
           build: .
           image: pv-case-review-backend:local
           ports:
             - "8080:8080"
           restart: "no"
           healthcheck:
             test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/health"]
             interval: 30s
             timeout: 3s
             start_period: 20s
             retries: 3
       ```
     - `restart: "no"` chosen explicitly (not omitted) because `CaseStore` is in-memory only — auto-restart after crash would silently wipe state.
     - Verified via `docker compose up -d --build`: restart policy "no" confirmed via `docker inspect`, healthcheck reached "healthy", API reachable on port 8080. Cleaned up with `docker compose down`.

   - **`backend/ops/run.sh`** (new file, just created, NOT yet functionally tested)
     - Made executable via `chmod +x`; syntax verified via `bash -n` → "syntax OK".
     - Full content:
       ```bash
       #!/usr/bin/env bash
       set -euo pipefail

       SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
       BACKEND_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

       usage() {
         cat <<'EOF'
       Usage: ops/run.sh <command>

       Commands:
         build    Build the backend Docker image
         start    Start the backend (docker compose up -d)
         stop     Stop the backend (docker compose down)
         test     Run the backend test suite (./gradlew test)
         logs     Follow the backend container logs
         clean    Stop containers, remove built images, and clean Gradle build output
         --help   Show this help message
       EOF
       }

       require_docker() {
         if ! docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
           echo "Error: Docker doesn't seem to be running. Start Docker and try again." >&2
           exit 1
         fi
       }

       cmd_build() {
         require_docker
         (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose build)
       }

       cmd_start() {
         require_docker
         (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose up -d)
       }

       cmd_stop() {
         require_docker
         (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose down)
       }

       cmd_test() {
         (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && ./gradlew test)
       }

       cmd_logs() {
         require_docker
         (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose logs -f backend)
       }

       cmd_clean() {
         require_docker
         (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose down --rmi local --volumes --remove-orphans)
         (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && ./gradlew clean)
       }

       case "${1:-}" in
         --help|-h)
           usage
           ;;
         build)
           cmd_build
           ;;
         start)
           cmd_start
           ;;
         stop)
           cmd_stop
           ;;
         test)
           cmd_test
           ;;
         logs)
           cmd_logs
           ;;
         clean)
           cmd_clean
           ;;
         "")
           echo "Error: no command given." >&2
           usage
           exit 1
           ;;
         *)
           echo "Error: unknown command '$1'." >&2
           usage
           exit 1
           ;;
       esac
       ```
     - Decision: placed under `backend/ops/` (not repo root) for consistency with where `Dockerfile`/`docker-compose.yml` live, and because the session's working-directory context has been scoped to `backend/`.
     - Design decisions: `require_docker()` is called in build/start/stop/logs/clean but deliberately NOT in `test` (which only needs Gradle, not Docker) or `--help`. Subcommand dispatch happens via a `case` statement on `${1:-}`. No-args and unknown-command both print an error to stderr, show usage, and exit 1; `--help`/`-h` shows usage with implicit exit 0.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Perceived MergeService bug (from prior session, referenced in continuation)**: Was actually caused by my own flawed test command — a `curl | python3 -m json.tool || fallback-curl` pattern that double-POSTed against the non-idempotent follow-up endpoint. Fixed by restarting clean and re-testing with single, careful curl invocations (capture-then-pretty-print pattern instead of pipe-with-fallback).
   - **Test input mistakes (mine, not app bugs)**: Used camelCase JSON body (`caseId`/`fieldPath`) against the globally-agreed snake_case convention, causing false validation failures; and an unquoted `?` in a URL caused zsh glob-expansion errors. Both fixed by correcting my test commands, not the app.
   - **Real bug: duplicated validation message** — `"question question is required"`. Root cause: `GlobalExceptionHandler.handleValidation` prepends `error.getField()` to `error.getDefaultMessage()`, but the `@NotBlank` messages in `QueryRequest` already included the field name. Fixed by removing the redundant field name from the three `@NotBlank` messages (now just `"is required"`). User explicitly reported this and asked for the fix; verified live after fix — message now reads correctly (e.g., `"question is required"`, and combined: `"caseId is required; fieldPath is required; question is required"`).
   - **Orphaned background processes holding port 8080**: Occurred multiple times when restarting `./gradlew bootRun` for live verification — `pkill -f "gradlew bootRun"` / `pkill -f "PvReviewApplication"` didn't always kill the actual Java child process. Fixed each time by using `lsof -i :8080` to find the exact PID and `kill <PID>` directly, then confirming `lsof -i :8080` showed "port 8080 free" before restarting.
   - **Docker build failure**: `eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine` has no `arm64` manifest (this Mac is Apple Silicon). Confirmed via direct `docker pull eclipse-temurin:17-jre-alpine` → "no matching manifest for linux/arm64/v8 in the manifest list entries: no match for platform in manifest: not found". Verified `17-jdk-jammy` and `17-jre-jammy` both pull successfully for arm64; confirmed alpine variant only works with explicit `--platform linux/amd64`. Fixed by switching the runtime stage to `eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy` and updating the non-root user creation commands from Alpine syntax (`addgroup -S`/`adduser -S`) to Debian/Ubuntu syntax (`groupadd --system`/`useradd --system --gid`).
   - **Self-correction on comment style**: Initially wrote a multi-line Javadoc-style comment (`/** ... */`) for the `restoreCase` method explaining its ops-only purpose, then caught myself violating the "never write multi-paragraph docstrings or multi-line comment blocks" style rule and replaced it with a single-line `//` comment instead, before running tests.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Resolved the architectural ambiguity around how `backup.sh`/`restore.sh` should interact with the API: introduced `GET /cases` (list) and `PUT /cases/{caseId}` (raw overwrite/upsert, bypassing merge) as a deliberate second write path distinct from the business-logic `POST /follow-ups` path. This was validated end-to-end with a real backup→mutate→restore→diff round trip showing byte-for-byte identical output.
   - Resolved Docker multi-platform compatibility issues (arm64 vs amd64) for the Eclipse Temurin Alpine image by switching to the Jammy variant, with verification that `wget`/`curl` are still available for the HEALTHCHECK in the new base image.
   - Resolved the restart-policy question for `docker-compose.yml` by reasoning specifically about this app's lack of persistence (in-memory `CaseStore`), recommending `restart: "no"` rather than generic Docker best-practice defaults.
   - No unresolved/ongoing troubleshooting at the time of this summary — `ops/run.sh` was just created and syntax-checked but not yet functionally exercised.

6. All user messages (verbatim, excluding tool results):
   - "Can u explain me the tests that you have written"
   - "One small thing — the validation error message for a missing question field comes back as \"question question is required\" — the field name is being prepended twice. Can you fix that?"
   - "One small cleanup I want  when I hit GET /cases/PV-2026-0451 on the baseline case, every field is returning \"status\": null and \"previous_value\": null in the JSON. Can we configure Jackson to not include null fields in the response? They should just be omitted when null."
   - "Can you write the README for the backend? The brief asks for 4 curl examples, one per main endpoint. Use the actual case ID PV-2026-0451.\n\nAlso add a short section explaining the merge behavior and the RETAINED status decision.\n\nKeep it practical and easy  like something that actually easily readable"
   - "I'm thinking about how backup.sh and restore.sh will work and I realized there's a problem.\n\nbackup.sh needs to save all cases but right now there's no way to list all cases — only GET /cases/{id} which needs a known ID upfront. And restore.sh needs to PUT cases back exactly as they were, but if I use the follow-up endpoint it'll run merge logic and mess things up.\n\nHow would you handle this?"
   - "Yeah exactly, that's what I was thinking too. Go ahead and add both. \n\nAnd yes, make it clear in the code comments that PUT /cases/{caseId} is ops-only — not a normal update path."
   - "Okay starting Phase 1B now. Let's do the Dockerfile first.\n\nI need a multi-stage build — one stage for building the jar, one stage for running it. Runtime should use JRE not JDK to keep the image small. Non-root user in the runtime stage. Pinned base image versions, not :latest.\n\nAlso add a HEALTHCHECK that hits /health.\n\nWhat base images would you pick for Java 17?"
   - "Wait, before backup.sh — let's do docker-compose.yml first. The brief asks for it separately. Just the backend service, include a healthcheck. What should restart policy be for a local dev setup?"
   - "Now let's do ops/run.sh first before backup and restore.\nThe brief needs subcommands: build, start, stop, test, logs, clean, and --help. Should fail gracefully if Docker isn't running."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Functionally test every subcommand of `backend/ops/run.sh` (build, start, stop, test, logs, clean, --help, unknown-command error, no-args error) and specifically verify the "fail gracefully if Docker isn't running" requirement — e.g., by simulating a non-functional `docker` binary via a temporary PATH override (without touching the real Docker daemon) and confirming `require_docker()` produces a clean error message and exit code 1, not a raw stack trace.
   - After `ops/run.sh` is verified, the user has stated `backup.sh` and `restore.sh` are next (explicitly deferred twice: "before backup.sh" and "before backup and restore").
   - Phase 2 (React frontend) has not been started and was not discussed in this session.

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before this summary request, I had just created `backend/ops/run.sh` per the user's explicit request: "Now let's do ops/run.sh first before backup and restore. The brief needs subcommands: build, start, stop, test, logs, clean, and --help. Should fail gracefully if Docker isn't running." I wrote the full script (shown in full in section 3 above), made it executable with `chmod +x /Users/ashitaairan/Documents/pv-case-review/backend/ops/run.sh`, and confirmed syntax validity with `bash -n /Users/ashitaairan/Documents/pv-case-review/backend/ops/run.sh && echo "syntax OK"`, which printed `syntax OK`. No functional testing of the script's subcommands had been performed yet — this is exactly where the conversation was cut off, before I could verify build/start/stop/test/logs/clean/--help all work correctly and before verifying the graceful-failure-when-Docker-isn't-running behavior.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Functionally verify `backend/ops/run.sh` end-to-end before considering it done, following the pattern established throughout this session (write, then live-verify every claim). Specifically:
   - Run `./ops/run.sh --help` and confirm usage prints correctly.
   - Run `./ops/run.sh build`, `./ops/run.sh start`, confirm the container starts and `/health` responds, `./ops/run.sh logs` (briefly, with a timeout since it follows by default), `./ops/run.sh stop`, `./ops/run.sh test` (runs `./gradlew test`), `./ops/run.sh clean` (and confirm the compose-built image and Gradle `build/` directory are actually removed afterward).
   - Test the no-args and unknown-command error paths (both should print an error + usage to stderr and exit 1).
   - Test the "fail gracefully if Docker isn't running" requirement by temporarily shadowing the `docker` command in `PATH` within a subshell (so the real Docker daemon is never touched) and confirming `require_docker()` triggers the clean error message and exit code 1 rather than a raw/confusing failure.
   This directly continues the task in progress and was the explicit, most recent, not-yet-confirmed-complete request from the user — no tangential or already-completed work should be revisited without the user's go-ahead.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/ashitaairan/.claude/projects/-Users-ashitaairan-Documents-pv-case-review/dbea81d5-9c8d-465e-934a-a8857a479d61.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

file.content:
services:
  backend:
    build: .
    image: pv-case-review-backend:local
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    restart: "no"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      start_period: 20s
      retries: 3

# ---- Build stage ----
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-jammy AS build
WORKDIR /app

COPY gradlew settings.gradle build.gradle ./
COPY gradle ./gradle
RUN ./gradlew dependencies --no-daemon

COPY src ./src
RUN ./gradlew bootJar --no-daemon

# ---- Runtime stage ----
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

RUN groupadd --system pvreview && useradd --system --gid pvreview pvreview
COPY --from=build /app/build/libs/*.jar app.jar
RUN chown pvreview:pvreview app.jar
USER pvreview

EXPOSE 8080
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=20s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

build/
.gradle/
out/
*.log
.idea/
*.iml

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
BACKEND_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
BACKUP_DIR="$BACKEND_DIR/backups"

BASE_URL="${PV_BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"

log() {
  echo "[$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)] $*" >&2
}

require_cmd() {
  if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Error: required command '$1' not found on PATH."
    exit 1
  fi
}

require_cmd curl
require_cmd jq

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

timestamp="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
dest="$BACKUP_DIR/cases-${timestamp}.json"
tmp_file="$(mktemp "$BACKUP_DIR/.cases-tmp.XXXXXXXX")"

cleanup() {
  rm -f "$tmp_file"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting backup from $BASE_URL/cases"

http_code="$(curl -sS -w '%{http_code}' -o "$tmp_file" "$BASE_URL/cases" || true)"

if [[ "$http_code" != "200" ]]; then
  log "Error: GET $BASE_URL/cases failed (HTTP ${http_code:-no response}). Is the backend running?"
  exit 1
fi

if ! jq empty "$tmp_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  log "Error: response body is not valid JSON. Aborting backup."
  exit 1
fi

case_count="$(jq 'length' "$tmp_file")"
mv "$tmp_file" "$dest"
trap - EXIT

log "Backup complete: ${case_count} case(s) written to $dest"

content.text: Found a real bug: two runs within the same UTC second produced the same timestamp, and the second `mv` silently overwrote the first backup — a quiet data-loss case that "safe to cron" should not allow. Fixing it by making the temp file's mktemp-guaranteed-unique suffix part of the final filename, so collisions are structurally impossible.

input.old_string:
timestamp="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
dest="$BACKUP_DIR/cases-${timestamp}.json"
tmp_file="$(mktemp "$BACKUP_DIR/.cases-tmp.XXXXXXXX")"
input.new_string:
timestamp="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
tmp_file="$(mktemp "$BACKUP_DIR/.cases-tmp.XXXXXXXX")"
unique="${tmp_file##*.cases-tmp.}"
dest="$BACKUP_DIR/cases-${timestamp}-${unique}.json"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
BACKEND_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
BACKUP_DIR="$BACKEND_DIR/backups"

BASE_URL="${PV_BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"

log() {
  echo "[$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)] $*" >&2
}

require_cmd() {
  if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Error: required command '$1' not found on PATH."
    exit 1
  fi
}

require_cmd curl
require_cmd jq

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

timestamp="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
dest="$BACKUP_DIR/cases-${timestamp}.json"
tmp_file="$(mktemp "$BACKUP_DIR/.cases-tmp.XXXXXXXX")"

cleanup() {
  rm -f "$tmp_file"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting backup from $BASE_URL/cases"

http_code="$(curl -sS -w '%{http_code}' -o "$tmp_file" "$BASE_URL/cases" || true)"

if [[ "$http_code" != "200" ]]; then
  log "Error: GET $BASE_URL/cases failed (HTTP ${http_code:-no response}). Is the backend running?"
  exit 1
fi

if ! jq empty "$tmp_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  log "Error: response body is not valid JSON. Aborting backup."
  exit 1
fi

case_count="$(jq 'length' "$tmp_file")"
mv "$tmp_file" "$dest"
trap - EXIT

log "Backup complete: ${case_count} case(s) written to $dest"

content.text:
`backend/ops/backup.sh` is done and fully verified:

```bash
BASE_URL="${PV_BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"   # overridable, defaults to the published port
```

| Behavior | Verified |
|---|---|
| Backend down | Clean stderr message + exit `1`, no stray temp files |
| Backend up | Fetches `GET /cases`, writes `backups/cases-<UTC timestamp>-<random>.json` |
| Content fidelity | Byte-identical to live `GET /cases` (diffed) |
| CWD independence | Ran correctly from `/tmp` — safe for cron's unspecified working directory |
| Same-second collisions | Fixed a real bug I caught in testing: 3 rapid runs initially collided on the timestamp and silently overwrote each other via `mv`. Fixed by folding `mktemp`'s unique suffix into the final filename — collisions are now structurally impossible while filenames stay timestamp-sortable |
| Missing `jq`/`curl` | Graceful `require_cmd` check, exit `1`, no raw "command not found" leak |
| Atomicity | Writes to a temp file first, validates HTTP 200 + valid JSON, then `mv`s into place — a killed/failed run never leaves a partial or corrupt backup |
| Logging | Every line timestamped (UTC, ISO 8601) on stderr, per the brief |

`backups/` was already gitignored. Next up: `restore.sh`.

message.content:
Now restore.sh. It should take a backup file as argument, 
PUT each case back using PUT /cases/{caseId}, and support a --dry-run flag 
that shows what would happen without making changes. Should be idempotent.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BASE_URL="${PV_BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"

DRY_RUN=false
BACKUP_FILE=""

usage() {
  cat <<'EOF'
Usage: ops/restore.sh [--dry-run] <backup-file>

Restores cases from a backup JSON file (as written by ops/backup.sh) using
PUT /cases/{caseId} -- a raw overwrite that bypasses merge logic entirely.
Re-running with the same file is a no-op: PUT always sets the exact same
state, it never merges, so restoring twice leaves the case unchanged.

  --dry-run   Show what would be restored without making any changes
EOF
}

log() {
  echo "[$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)] $*" >&2
}

require_cmd() {
  if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Error: required command '$1' not found on PATH."
    exit 1
  fi
}

for arg in "$@"; do
  case "$arg" in
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=true
      ;;
    --help|-h)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    -*)
      echo "Error: unknown option '$arg'." >&2
      usage
      exit 1
      ;;
    *)
      if [[ -n "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
        echo "Error: too many arguments." >&2
        usage
        exit 1
      fi
      BACKUP_FILE="$arg"
      ;;
  esac
done

if [[ -z "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
  echo "Error: missing required <backup-file> argument." >&2
  usage
  exit 1
fi

require_cmd curl
require_cmd jq

if [[ ! -f "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
  log "Error: backup file '$BACKUP_FILE' not found."
  exit 1
fi

if ! jq empty "$BACKUP_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  log "Error: '$BACKUP_FILE' is not valid JSON."
  exit 1
fi

if [[ "$(jq -r 'type' "$BACKUP_FILE")" != "array" ]]; then
  log "Error: '$BACKUP_FILE' does not contain a JSON array of cases."
  exit 1
fi

case_count="$(jq 'length' "$BACKUP_FILE")"

if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == true ]]; then
  log "Dry run: $case_count case(s) found in $BACKUP_FILE"
else
  log "Restoring $case_count case(s) from $BACKUP_FILE"
fi

restored=0
while IFS= read -r case_json; do
  case_id="$(jq -r '.case_id // empty' <<<"$case_json")"
  if [[ -z "$case_id" ]]; then
    log "Error: found a case in the backup with no case_id. Aborting."
    exit 1
  fi

  if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == true ]]; then
    log "[DRY RUN] would PUT $BASE_URL/cases/$case_id"
    restored=$((restored + 1))
    continue
  fi

  response="$(curl -sS -X PUT -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    --data-binary "$case_json" -w $'\n%{http_code}' "$BASE_URL/cases/$case_id" || true)"
  http_code="${response##*$'\n'}"
  body="${response%$'\n'*}"

  if [[ "$http_code" != "200" ]]; then
    log "Error: PUT $BASE_URL/cases/$case_id failed (HTTP ${http_code:-no response}): $body"
    exit 1
  fi

  log "Restored $case_id"
  restored=$((restored + 1))
done < <(jq -c '.[]' "$BACKUP_FILE")

if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == true ]]; then
  log "Dry run complete: $restored case(s) would be restored"
else
  log "Restore complete: $restored case(s) restored from $BACKUP_FILE"
fi

content.text:
`backend/ops/restore.sh` is done and fully verified end-to-end:

| Behavior | Verified |
|---|---|
| `--dry-run` | Logs intent (`[DRY RUN] would PUT ...`) without any HTTP call — live state confirmed unchanged afterward |
| Real restore | `PUT /cases/{caseId}` per case — restored state matched the backup file byte-for-byte (diffed) |
| Idempotency | Restoring the same file twice produced identical live state both times — no drift, since `PUT` is always a raw overwrite, never a merge |
| Round-trip with diverging state | Mutated case to v2 → backed up → mutated to v3 → dry-run (no change) → restore (back to v2) → restore again (still v2) |
| `--help` / no args / unknown option | Usage to stderr/stdout as appropriate, correct exit codes (`0` for `--help`, `1` for errors) |
| Bad input file | Nonexistent file, invalid JSON, and valid-but-non-array JSON all caught with clear messages before any HTTP call |
| Backend down | Clean error + exit `1`, no raw curl/stack trace leak |

Ops tooling for Phase 1B is now complete: `Dockerfile`, `.dockerignore`, `docker-compose.yml`, `ops/run.sh`, `ops/backup.sh`, `ops/restore.sh` — all individually live-tested. Phase 2 (React frontend) is next whenever you're ready.

1	# PV Case Review — Backend
2	
3	Spring Boot service for reviewing pharmacovigilance (PV) cases and merging in
4	follow-up reports. No database — cases and reviewer queries live in memory
5	for the lifetime of the process.
6	
7	## Stack
8	
9	- Java 17, Spring Boot 3.3, Gradle (wrapper included)
10	- Lombok for model/DTO boilerplate
11	- JSON request/response bodies use `snake_case` everywhere (`case_id`,
12	  `field_path`, etc.) via a global Jackson naming strategy — fields that are
13	  `null` are omitted from responses rather than serialized as `null`
14	
15	## Running it
16	
17	```bash
18	./gradlew bootRun
19	```
20	
21	The app starts on `http://localhost:8080`. On startup it bootstraps one case,
22	**`PV-2026-0451`**, from `src/main/resources/data/case_v1.json`, so there's
23	something to query immediately — no seeding step required.
24	
25	## Running the tests
26	
27	```bash
28	./gradlew test
29	```
30	
31	## The four main endpoints
32	
33	### 1. Get a case
34	
35	```bash
36	curl http://localhost:8080/cases/PV-2026-0451
37	```
38	
39	```json
40	{
41	  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
42	  "version": 1,
43	  "case_classification": "non-significant",
44	  "extracted_at": "2026-04-08T09:14:00Z",
45	  "source_document": "initial_report_PV-2026-0451.pdf",
46	  "sections": {
47	    "patient": {
48	      "age": { "value": "62", "confidence": 0.91, "source": "p.2 §1" },
49	      "weight_kg": { "value": "78", "confidence": 0.85, "source": "p.3 §2" }
50	    }
51	  },
52	  "missing_fields": []
53	}
54	```
55	
56	Unknown case id → `404` with an `ErrorResponse` body.
57	
58	### 2. Apply a follow-up report (merge)
59	
60	```bash
61	curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/cases/PV-2026-0451/follow-ups \
62	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
63	  -d '{
64	    "case_classification": "significant",
65	    "sections": {
66	      "patient": {
67	        "age": { "value": "63", "confidence": 0.90, "source": "p.2 §1" }
68	      },
69	      "adverse_event": {
70	        "severity_grade": { "value": "Grade 2", "confidence": 0.83, "source": "p.4 §3" }
71	      },
72	      "lab_results": {
73	        "ck_level": { "value": "450 U/L", "confidence": 0.89, "source": "p.6 §1" }
74	      }
75	    },
76	    "missing_fields": ["patient.weight_kg"]
77	  }'
78	```
79	
80	```json
81	{
82	  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
83	  "version": 2,
84	  "case_classification": "significant",
85	  "sections": {
86	    "patient": {
87	      "age": { "value": "63", "confidence": 0.90, "source": "p.2 §1", "status": "OVERRIDDEN", "previous_value": "62" },
88	      "weight_kg": { "value": "78", "confidence": 0.85, "source": "p.3 §2", "status": "RETAINED" }
89	    },
90	    "adverse_event": {
91	      "severity_grade": { "value": "Grade 2", "confidence": 0.83, "source": "p.4 §3", "status": "NEW" }
92	    },
93	    "lab_results": {
94	      "ck_level": { "value": "450 U/L", "confidence": 0.89, "source": "p.6 §1", "status": "NEW" }
95	    }
96	  },
97	  "missing_fields": ["patient.weight_kg"]
98	}
99	```
100	
101	`version` auto-increments if you don't supply one. Unknown case id → `404`;
102	malformed JSON body → `400`.
103	
104	### 3. Submit a reviewer query against a field
105	
106	```bash
107	curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/queries \
108	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
109	  -d '{
110	    "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
111	    "field_path": "patient.age",
112	    "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?"
113	  }'
114	```
115	
116	```json
117	{
118	  "id": "6f9ee839-db97-49e4-90e6-260761a210c1",
119	  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
120	  "field_path": "patient.age",
121	  "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?",
122	  "created_at": "2026-06-21T10:04:53.312608Z"
123	}
124	```
125	
126	Any blank field → `400` (e.g. `"question is required"`).
127	
128	### 4. List queries for a case
129	
130	```bash
131	curl "http://localhost:8080/queries?caseId=PV-2026-0451"
132	```
133	
134	```json
135	[
136	  {
137	    "id": "6f9ee839-db97-49e4-90e6-260761a210c1",
138	    "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
139	    "field_path": "patient.age",
140	    "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?",
141	    "created_at": "2026-06-21T10:04:53.312608Z"
142	  }
143	]
144	```
145	
146	Note: `caseId` here is a URL query parameter, not a JSON body field, so it
147	stays camelCase — the `snake_case` rule only applies to JSON bodies.
148	
149	*(There's also a `GET /health` liveness check, returning `{"status": "UP"}`.)*
150	
151	## Merge behavior
152	
153	Every field on a case carries a `status` describing how it got its current
154	value, computed each time a follow-up is applied:
155	
156	| Status | Meaning |
157	|---|---|
158	| `NEW` | Field (or whole section) didn't exist before; the follow-up introduced it. |
159	| `OVERRIDDEN` | Field existed before with a different value; the follow-up replaced it. `previous_value` holds what it used to be. |
160	| `UNCHANGED` | Field existed before with the *same* value; the follow-up just re-confirmed it. |
161	| `RETAINED` | Field existed before and the follow-up **didn't mention it at all**; the stored value carries forward as-is. |
162	
163	### Why `RETAINED` exists
164	
165	A follow-up report only contains the fields the extractor found evidence for
166	in *that* document — it's a partial payload, not a full restatement of the
167	case. If a field is missing from the follow-up, that's almost always because
168	the new document simply didn't re-cover it, not because it should be erased.
169	Treating "absent from follow-up" the same as "explicitly removed" would
170	silently destroy data from earlier reports, which is the wrong default for
171	PV case data where every value is sourced and audited.
172	
173	So instead of overwriting or dropping anything, the merge keeps the stored
174	field's `value`/`confidence`/`source` untouched and tags it `RETAINED`,
175	making it visible to a reviewer as "carried forward, not reconfirmed" rather
176	than indistinguishable from a freshly-extracted field. This applies at both
177	granularities: a single field can be retained inside an otherwise-updated
178	section (e.g. `patient.weight_kg` above), and a whole section omitted from
179	the follow-up is retained field-by-field, not dropped wholesale.
180	
181	## Error responses
182	
183	All errors share one shape:
184	
185	```json
186	{
187	  "status": 404,
188	  "error": "Not Found",
189	  "message": "No case found with id UNKNOWN-ID",
190	  "path": "/cases/UNKNOWN-ID",
191	  "timestamp": "2026-06-21T10:07:46.857813Z"
192	}
193	```
194	
195	| Situation | Status |
196	|---|---|
197	| Unknown `caseId` | `404` |
198	| Malformed JSON body | `400` |
199	| Missing/blank required field (e.g. in `/queries`) | `400` |
200	| Missing required query param (e.g. `caseId` on `GET /queries`) | `400` |
201

file.content:
# PV Case Review — Backend

Spring Boot service for reviewing pharmacovigilance (PV) cases and merging in
follow-up reports. No database — cases and reviewer queries live in memory
for the lifetime of the process.

## Stack

- Java 17, Spring Boot 3.3, Gradle (wrapper included)
- Lombok for model/DTO boilerplate
- JSON request/response bodies use `snake_case` everywhere (`case_id`,
  `field_path`, etc.) via a global Jackson naming strategy — fields that are
  `null` are omitted from responses rather than serialized as `null`

## Running it

```bash
./gradlew bootRun
```

The app starts on `http://localhost:8080`. On startup it bootstraps one case,
**`PV-2026-0451`**, from `src/main/resources/data/case_v1.json`, so there's
something to query immediately — no seeding step required.

## Running the tests

```bash
./gradlew test
```

## The four main endpoints

### 1. Get a case

```bash
curl http://localhost:8080/cases/PV-2026-0451
```

```json
{
  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
  "version": 1,
  "case_classification": "non-significant",
  "extracted_at": "2026-04-08T09:14:00Z",
  "source_document": "initial_report_PV-2026-0451.pdf",
  "sections": {
    "patient": {
      "age": { "value": "62", "confidence": 0.91, "source": "p.2 §1" },
      "weight_kg": { "value": "78", "confidence": 0.85, "source": "p.3 §2" }
    }
  },
  "missing_fields": []
}
```

Unknown case id → `404` with an `ErrorResponse` body.

### 2. Apply a follow-up report (merge)

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/cases/PV-2026-0451/follow-ups \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "case_classification": "significant",
    "sections": {
      "patient": {
        "age": { "value": "63", "confidence": 0.90, "source": "p.2 §1" }
      },
      "adverse_event": {
        "severity_grade": { "value": "Grade 2", "confidence": 0.83, "source": "p.4 §3" }
      },
      "lab_results": {
        "ck_level": { "value": "450 U/L", "confidence": 0.89, "source": "p.6 §1" }
      }
    },
    "missing_fields": ["patient.weight_kg"]
  }'
```

```json
{
  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
  "version": 2,
  "case_classification": "significant",
  "sections": {
    "patient": {
      "age": { "value": "63", "confidence": 0.90, "source": "p.2 §1", "status": "OVERRIDDEN", "previous_value": "62" },
      "weight_kg": { "value": "78", "confidence": 0.85, "source": "p.3 §2", "status": "RETAINED" }
    },
    "adverse_event": {
      "severity_grade": { "value": "Grade 2", "confidence": 0.83, "source": "p.4 §3", "status": "NEW" }
    },
    "lab_results": {
      "ck_level": { "value": "450 U/L", "confidence": 0.89, "source": "p.6 §1", "status": "NEW" }
    }
  },
  "missing_fields": ["patient.weight_kg"]
}
```

`version` auto-increments if you don't supply one. Unknown case id → `404`;
malformed JSON body → `400`.

### 3. Submit a reviewer query against a field

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/queries \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
    "field_path": "patient.age",
    "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?"
  }'
```

```json
{
  "id": "6f9ee839-db97-49e4-90e6-260761a210c1",
  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
  "field_path": "patient.age",
  "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?",
  "created_at": "2026-06-21T10:04:53.312608Z"
}
```

Any blank field → `400` (e.g. `"question is required"`).

### 4. List queries for a case

```bash
curl "http://localhost:8080/queries?caseId=PV-2026-0451"
```

```json
[
  {
    "id": "6f9ee839-db97-49e4-90e6-260761a210c1",
    "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
    "field_path": "patient.age",
    "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?",
    "created_at": "2026-06-21T10:04:53.312608Z"
  }
]
```

Note: `caseId` here is a URL query parameter, not a JSON body field, so it
stays camelCase — the `snake_case` rule only applies to JSON bodies.

*(There's also a `GET /health` liveness check, returning `{"status": "UP"}`.)*

## Merge behavior

Every field on a case carries a `status` describing how it got its current
value, computed each time a follow-up is applied:

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `NEW` | Field (or whole section) didn't exist before; the follow-up introduced it. |
| `OVERRIDDEN` | Field existed before with a different value; the follow-up replaced it. `previous_value` holds what it used to be. |
| `UNCHANGED` | Field existed before with the *same* value; the follow-up just re-confirmed it. |
| `RETAINED` | Field existed before and the follow-up **didn't mention it at all**; the stored value carries forward as-is. |

### Why `RETAINED` exists

A follow-up report only contains the fields the extractor found evidence for
in *that* document — it's a partial payload, not a full restatement of the
case. If a field is missing from the follow-up, that's almost always because
the new document simply didn't re-cover it, not because it should be erased.
Treating "absent from follow-up" the same as "explicitly removed" would
silently destroy data from earlier reports, which is the wrong default for
PV case data where every value is sourced and audited.

So instead of overwriting or dropping anything, the merge keeps the stored
field's `value`/`confidence`/`source` untouched and tags it `RETAINED`,
making it visible to a reviewer as "carried forward, not reconfirmed" rather
than indistinguishable from a freshly-extracted field. This applies at both
granularities: a single field can be retained inside an otherwise-updated
section (e.g. `patient.weight_kg` above), and a whole section omitted from
the follow-up is retained field-by-field, not dropped wholesale.

## Error responses

All errors share one shape:

```json
{
  "status": 404,
  "error": "Not Found",
  "message": "No case found with id UNKNOWN-ID",
  "path": "/cases/UNKNOWN-ID",
  "timestamp": "2026-06-21T10:07:46.857813Z"
}
```

| Situation | Status |
|---|---|
| Unknown `caseId` | `404` |
| Malformed JSON body | `400` |
| Missing/blank required field (e.g. in `/queries`) | `400` |
| Missing required query param (e.g. `caseId` on `GET /queries`) | `400` |

  build                          Build the backend Docker image
  start                          Start the backend (docker compose up -d)
  stop                           Stop the backend (docker compose down)
  test                           Run the backend test suite (./gradlew test)
  logs                           Follow the backend container logs
  clean                          Stop containers, remove built images, and clean Gradle build output
  backup                         Snapshot all cases to backups/cases-<timestamp>.json
  restore FILE=...               Restore cases from a backup file (PUT, bypasses merge)
  restore FILE=... DRY_RUN=1     Show what restore would do without making changes

Restores cases from a backup JSON file (as written by ops/backup.sh) using
PUT /cases/{caseId} -- a raw overwrite that bypasses merge logic entirely.
Re-running with the same file is a no-op: PUT always sets the exact same
state, it never merges, so restoring twice leaves the case unchanged.

  --dry-run   Show what would be restored without making any changes
make: *** [restore] Error 1
exit: 2

=== make help ===
Usage: make <target>

Targets:
  build                          Build the backend Docker image
  start                          Start the backend (docker compose up -d)

input.old_string: | Missing required query param (e.g. `caseId` on `GET /queries`) | `400` |
input.new_string:
| Missing required query param (e.g. `caseId` on `GET /queries`) | `400` |

## Operations

Running via Docker, plus backup/restore tooling under `ops/`. None of this
needs the JVM or Gradle on the host — only Docker.

### Running via Docker

```bash
docker compose up -d --build
```

Builds the image (multi-stage: JDK to compile, JRE to run, non-root user)
and starts the backend on `http://localhost:8080` — same app as
`./gradlew bootRun`, just containerized. `docker-compose.yml` sets
`restart: "no"` on purpose: `CaseStore` is in-memory, so an auto-restarting
container would come back up with an empty store, silently losing whatever
was there before the crash.

### `ops/run.sh` and the Makefile

`ops/run.sh` wraps Docker Compose and Gradle with the usual lifecycle
subcommands; the `Makefile` just calls it so you can use whichever you
prefer:

```bash
./ops/run.sh build   /  make build    # docker compose build
./ops/run.sh start   /  make start    # docker compose up -d
./ops/run.sh stop    /  make stop     # docker compose down
./ops/run.sh test    /  make test     # ./gradlew test (doesn't need Docker)
./ops/run.sh logs    /  make logs     # follow container logs
./ops/run.sh clean   /  make clean    # stop containers, remove the image, ./gradlew clean
./ops/run.sh --help  /  make help
```

Every subcommand except `test` and `--help` checks `docker info` first and
fails with a one-line error if Docker isn't running, instead of letting a
raw Docker error leak through.

### Backups and restores

`backup.sh` fetches every case from a running backend and writes them to a
single timestamped, gitignored file:

```bash
make backup
# [2026-06-21T11:51:11Z] Starting backup from http://localhost:8080/cases
# [2026-06-21T11:51:11Z] Backup complete: 1 case(s) written to backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json
```

`restore.sh` takes a backup file and puts each case back exactly as it was,
via `PUT /cases/{caseId}` — not the follow-up endpoint, so merge logic never
runs. Use `--dry-run` to see what it would do first:

```bash
make restore FILE=backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json DRY_RUN=1
make restore FILE=backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json
```

Restoring is idempotent: `PUT` always sets the case to exactly the state in
the file, so restoring the same file twice leaves the case unchanged rather
than compounding anything.

### Why `PUT /cases/{caseId}` exists separately from `POST /follow-ups`

These two write paths look similar but do very different things:

| Endpoint | Purpose | Runs merge logic? |
|---|---|---|
| `POST /cases/{caseId}/follow-ups` | Normal application flow — apply a new follow-up report to a case | Yes — computes `NEW`/`OVERRIDDEN`/`UNCHANGED`/`RETAINED` per field |
| `PUT /cases/{caseId}` | **Ops only**, used by `restore.sh` to put a case back exactly as a backup recorded it | No — raw overwrite, verbatim |

If `restore.sh` used the follow-up endpoint instead, the restored snapshot
would get merged against whatever the live case currently looks like, which
could change field statuses and values instead of recreating the backed-up
state exactly. `PUT` exists so a restore is a true rollback, not another
merge — it isn't meant to be used as a general "update a case" endpoint.

`GET /cases` (list all cases) exists for the same reason: `backup.sh` needs
to discover every case id without already knowing them upfront.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# PV Case Review — Backend

Spring Boot service for reviewing pharmacovigilance (PV) cases and merging in
follow-up reports. No database — cases and reviewer queries live in memory
for the lifetime of the process.

## Stack

- Java 17, Spring Boot 3.3, Gradle (wrapper included)
- Lombok for model/DTO boilerplate
- JSON request/response bodies use `snake_case` everywhere (`case_id`,
  `field_path`, etc.) via a global Jackson naming strategy — fields that are
  `null` are omitted from responses rather than serialized as `null`

## Running it

```bash
./gradlew bootRun
```

The app starts on `http://localhost:8080`. On startup it bootstraps one case,
**`PV-2026-0451`**, from `src/main/resources/data/case_v1.json`, so there's
something to query immediately — no seeding step required.

## Running the tests

```bash
./gradlew test
```

## The four main endpoints

### 1. Get a case

```bash
curl http://localhost:8080/cases/PV-2026-0451
```

```json
{
  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
  "version": 1,
  "case_classification": "non-significant",
  "extracted_at": "2026-04-08T09:14:00Z",
  "source_document": "initial_report_PV-2026-0451.pdf",
  "sections": {
    "patient": {
      "age": { "value": "62", "confidence": 0.91, "source": "p.2 §1" },
      "weight_kg": { "value": "78", "confidence": 0.85, "source": "p.3 §2" }
    }
  },
  "missing_fields": []
}
```

Unknown case id → `404` with an `ErrorResponse` body.

### 2. Apply a follow-up report (merge)

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/cases/PV-2026-0451/follow-ups \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "case_classification": "significant",
    "sections": {
      "patient": {
        "age": { "value": "63", "confidence": 0.90, "source": "p.2 §1" }
      },
      "adverse_event": {
        "severity_grade": { "value": "Grade 2", "confidence": 0.83, "source": "p.4 §3" }
      },
      "lab_results": {
        "ck_level": { "value": "450 U/L", "confidence": 0.89, "source": "p.6 §1" }
      }
    },
    "missing_fields": ["patient.weight_kg"]
  }'
```

```json
{
  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
  "version": 2,
  "case_classification": "significant",
  "sections": {
    "patient": {
      "age": { "value": "63", "confidence": 0.90, "source": "p.2 §1", "status": "OVERRIDDEN", "previous_value": "62" },
      "weight_kg": { "value": "78", "confidence": 0.85, "source": "p.3 §2", "status": "RETAINED" }
    },
    "adverse_event": {
      "severity_grade": { "value": "Grade 2", "confidence": 0.83, "source": "p.4 §3", "status": "NEW" }
    },
    "lab_results": {
      "ck_level": { "value": "450 U/L", "confidence": 0.89, "source": "p.6 §1", "status": "NEW" }
    }
  },
  "missing_fields": ["patient.weight_kg"]
}
```

`version` auto-increments if you don't supply one. Unknown case id → `404`;
malformed JSON body → `400`.

### 3. Submit a reviewer query against a field

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/queries \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
    "field_path": "patient.age",
    "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?"
  }'
```

```json
{
  "id": "6f9ee839-db97-49e4-90e6-260761a210c1",
  "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
  "field_path": "patient.age",
  "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?",
  "created_at": "2026-06-21T10:04:53.312608Z"
}
```

Any blank field → `400` (e.g. `"question is required"`).

### 4. List queries for a case

```bash
curl "http://localhost:8080/queries?caseId=PV-2026-0451"
```

```json
[
  {
    "id": "6f9ee839-db97-49e4-90e6-260761a210c1",
    "case_id": "PV-2026-0451",
    "field_path": "patient.age",
    "question": "Why did age change from 62 to 63?",
    "created_at": "2026-06-21T10:04:53.312608Z"
  }
]
```

Note: `caseId` here is a URL query parameter, not a JSON body field, so it
stays camelCase — the `snake_case` rule only applies to JSON bodies.

*(There's also a `GET /health` liveness check, returning `{"status": "UP"}`.)*

## Merge behavior

Every field on a case carries a `status` describing how it got its current
value, computed each time a follow-up is applied:

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `NEW` | Field (or whole section) didn't exist before; the follow-up introduced it. |
| `OVERRIDDEN` | Field existed before with a different value; the follow-up replaced it. `previous_value` holds what it used to be. |
| `UNCHANGED` | Field existed before with the *same* value; the follow-up just re-confirmed it. |
| `RETAINED` | Field existed before and the follow-up **didn't mention it at all**; the stored value carries forward as-is. |

### Why `RETAINED` exists

A follow-up report only contains the fields the extractor found evidence for
in *that* document — it's a partial payload, not a full restatement of the
case. If a field is missing from the follow-up, that's almost always because
the new document simply didn't re-cover it, not because it should be erased.
Treating "absent from follow-up" the same as "explicitly removed" would
silently destroy data from earlier reports, which is the wrong default for
PV case data where every value is sourced and audited.

So instead of overwriting or dropping anything, the merge keeps the stored
field's `value`/`confidence`/`source` untouched and tags it `RETAINED`,
making it visible to a reviewer as "carried forward, not reconfirmed" rather
than indistinguishable from a freshly-extracted field. This applies at both
granularities: a single field can be retained inside an otherwise-updated
section (e.g. `patient.weight_kg` above), and a whole section omitted from
the follow-up is retained field-by-field, not dropped wholesale.

## Error responses

All errors share one shape:

```json
{
  "status": 404,
  "error": "Not Found",
  "message": "No case found with id UNKNOWN-ID",
  "path": "/cases/UNKNOWN-ID",
  "timestamp": "2026-06-21T10:07:46.857813Z"
}
```

| Situation | Status |
|---|---|
| Unknown `caseId` | `404` |
| Malformed JSON body | `400` |
| Missing/blank required field (e.g. in `/queries`) | `400` |
| Missing required query param (e.g. `caseId` on `GET /queries`) | `400` |

lines: +Running via Docker, plus backup/restore tooling under `ops/`. None of this
+needs the JVM or Gradle on the host — only Docker.
+Builds the image (multi-stage: JDK to compile, JRE to run, non-root user)
+and starts the backend on `http://localhost:8080` — same app as
+`restart: "no"` on purpose: `CaseStore` is in-memory, so an auto-restarting
+container would come back up with an empty store, silently losing whatever
+`ops/run.sh` wraps Docker Compose and Gradle with the usual lifecycle
+subcommands; the `Makefile` just calls it so you can use whichever you
+./ops/run.sh build   /  make build    # docker compose build
+./ops/run.sh start   /  make start    # docker compose up -d
+./ops/run.sh stop    /  make stop     # docker compose down
+./ops/run.sh test    /  make test     # ./gradlew test (doesn't need Docker)
+./ops/run.sh logs    /  make logs     # follow container logs
+./ops/run.sh clean   /  make clean    # stop containers, remove the image, ./gradlew clean
+Every subcommand except `test` and `--help` checks `docker info` first and
+fails with a one-line error if Docker isn't running, instead of letting a
+`backup.sh` fetches every case from a running backend and writes them to a
+`restore.sh` takes a backup file and puts each case back exactly as it was,
+via `PUT /cases/{caseId}` — not the follow-up endpoint, so merge logic never
+runs. Use `--dry-run` to see what it would do first:
+Restoring is idempotent: `PUT` always sets the case to exactly the state in
+the file, so restoring the same file twice leaves the case unchanged rather
+These two write paths look similar but do very different things:
+| `POST /cases/{caseId}/follow-ups` | Normal application flow — apply a new follow-up report to a case | Yes — computes `NEW`/`OVERRIDDEN`/`UNCHANGED`/`RETAINED` per field |
+| `PUT /cases/{caseId}` | **Ops only**, used by `restore.sh` to put a case back exactly as a backup recorded it | No — raw overwrite, verbatim |
+If `restore.sh` used the follow-up endpoint instead, the restored snapshot
+would get merged against whatever the live case currently looks like, which
+could change field statuses and values instead of recreating the backed-up
+state exactly. `PUT` exists so a restore is a true rollback, not another
+merge — it isn't meant to be used as a general "update a case" endpoint.
+`GET /cases` (list all cases) exists for the same reason: `backup.sh` needs
+to discover every case id without already knowing them upfront.

Both deliverables are done and verified:

**`backend/Makefile`** — targets `build`, `start`, `stop`, `test`, `logs`, `clean`, `backup`, `restore`, `help` (default). All tested live through a full lifecycle (build → start → backup → restore dry-run → restore real → test → logs → stop → clean), including `make restore` with no `FILE` correctly delegating to `restore.sh`'s own graceful error.

**README "Operations" section** — added after "Error responses", covering: running via `docker compose`, the `restart: "no"` rationale, `ops/run.sh`/Makefile mapping, backup/restore usage with idempotency note, and a table explaining why `PUT /cases/{caseId}` (ops-only rollback) is a separate path from `POST /follow-ups` (normal merge flow), plus why `GET /cases` exists.

Phase 1B is now fully complete: Dockerfile, `.dockerignore`, `docker-compose.yml`, `ops/run.sh`, `ops/backup.sh`, `ops/restore.sh`, `Makefile`, and README docs — all individually live-tested. Phase 2 (React frontend) is next whenever you're ready.

FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jdk-jammy AS build
WORKDIR /app

COPY gradlew settings.gradle build.gradle ./
COPY gradle ./gradle
RUN ./gradlew dependencies --no-daemon

COPY src ./src
RUN ./gradlew bootJar --no-daemon

# ---- Runtime stage ----
FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre-jammy AS runtime
WORKDIR /app

RUN groupadd --system pvreview && useradd --system --gid pvreview pvreview
COPY --from=build /app/build/libs/*.jar app.jar
RUN chown pvreview:pvreview app.jar
USER pvreview

EXPOSE 8080
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=20s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
5	BACKEND_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
6	
7	usage() {
8	  cat <<'EOF'
9	Usage: ops/run.sh <command>
10	
11	Commands:
12	  build    Build the backend Docker image
13	  start    Start the backend (docker compose up -d)
14	  stop     Stop the backend (docker compose down)
15	  test     Run the backend test suite (./gradlew test)
16	  logs     Follow the backend container logs
17	  clean    Stop containers, remove built images, and clean Gradle build output
18	  --help   Show this help message
19	EOF
20	}
21	
22	require_docker() {
23	  if ! docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
24	    echo "Error: Docker doesn't seem to be running. Start Docker and try again." >&2
25	    exit 1
26	  fi
27	}
28	
29	cmd_build() {
30	  require_docker
31	  (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose build)
32	}
33	
34	cmd_start() {
35	  require_docker
36	  (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose up -d)
37	}
38	
39	cmd_stop() {
40	  require_docker
41	  (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose down)
42	}
43	
44	cmd_test() {
45	  (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && ./gradlew test)
46	}
47	
48	cmd_logs() {
49	  require_docker
50	  (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose logs -f backend)
51	}
52	
53	cmd_clean() {
54	  require_docker
55	  (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && docker compose down --rmi local --volumes --remove-orphans)
56	  (cd "$BACKEND_DIR" && ./gradlew clean)
57	}
58	
59	case "${1:-}" in
60	  --help|-h)
61	    usage
62	    ;;
63	  build)
64	    cmd_build
65	    ;;
66	  start)
67	    cmd_start
68	    ;;
69	  stop)
70	    cmd_stop
71	    ;;
72	  test)
73	    cmd_test
74	    ;;
75	  logs)
76	    cmd_logs
77	    ;;
78	  clean)
79	    cmd_clean
80	    ;;
81	  "")
82	    echo "Error: no command given." >&2
83	    usage
84	    exit 1
85	    ;;
86	  *)
87	    echo "Error: unknown command '$1'." >&2
88	    usage
89	    exit 1
90	    ;;
91	esac
92

content.text: Now `ops/backup.sh` — `backups/` should now be a root-level sibling of `ops/`, not nested under `backend/`.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
5	BACKEND_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
6	BACKUP_DIR="$BACKEND_DIR/backups"
7	
8	BASE_URL="${PV_BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
9	
10	log() {
11	  echo "[$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)] $*" >&2
12	}
13	
14	require_cmd() {
15	  if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
16	    log "Error: required command '$1' not found on PATH."
17	    exit 1
18	  fi
19	}
20	
21	require_cmd curl
22	require_cmd jq
23	
24	mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"
25	
26	timestamp="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
27	tmp_file="$(mktemp "$BACKUP_DIR/.cases-tmp.XXXXXXXX")"
28	unique="${tmp_file##*.cases-tmp.}"
29	dest="$BACKUP_DIR/cases-${timestamp}-${unique}.json"
30	
31	cleanup() {
32	  rm -f "$tmp_file"
33	}
34	trap cleanup EXIT
35	
36	log "Starting backup from $BASE_URL/cases"
37	
38	http_code="$(curl -sS -w '%{http_code}' -o "$tmp_file" "$BASE_URL/cases" || true)"
39	
40	if [[ "$http_code" != "200" ]]; then
41	  log "Error: GET $BASE_URL/cases failed (HTTP ${http_code:-no response}). Is the backend running?"
42	  exit 1
43	fi
44	
45	if ! jq empty "$tmp_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
46	  log "Error: response body is not valid JSON. Aborting backup."
47	  exit 1
48	fi
49	
50	case_count="$(jq 'length' "$tmp_file")"
51	mv "$tmp_file" "$dest"
52	trap - EXIT
53	
54	log "Backup complete: ${case_count} case(s) written to $dest"
55

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
BACKEND_DIR="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
BACKUP_DIR="$BACKEND_DIR/backups"

BASE_URL="${PV_BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"

log() {
  echo "[$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)] $*" >&2
}

require_cmd() {
  if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Error: required command '$1' not found on PATH."
    exit 1
  fi
}

require_cmd curl
require_cmd jq

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"

timestamp="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
tmp_file="$(mktemp "$BACKUP_DIR/.cases-tmp.XXXXXXXX")"
unique="${tmp_file##*.cases-tmp.}"
dest="$BACKUP_DIR/cases-${timestamp}-${unique}.json"

cleanup() {
  rm -f "$tmp_file"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting backup from $BASE_URL/cases"

http_code="$(curl -sS -w '%{http_code}' -o "$tmp_file" "$BASE_URL/cases" || true)"

if [[ "$http_code" != "200" ]]; then
  log "Error: GET $BASE_URL/cases failed (HTTP ${http_code:-no response}). Is the backend running?"
  exit 1
fi

if ! jq empty "$tmp_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  log "Error: response body is not valid JSON. Aborting backup."
  exit 1
fi

case_count="$(jq 'length' "$tmp_file")"
mv "$tmp_file" "$dest"
trap - EXIT

log "Backup complete: ${case_count} case(s) written to $dest"

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

BASE_URL="${PV_BACKEND_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"

DRY_RUN=false
BACKUP_FILE=""

usage() {
  cat <<'EOF'
Usage: ops/restore.sh [--dry-run] <backup-file>

Restores cases from a backup JSON file (as written by ops/backup.sh) using
PUT /cases/{caseId} -- a raw overwrite that bypasses merge logic entirely.
Re-running with the same file is a no-op: PUT always sets the exact same
state, it never merges, so restoring twice leaves the case unchanged.

  --dry-run   Show what would be restored without making any changes
EOF
}

log() {
  echo "[$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)] $*" >&2
}

require_cmd() {
  if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "Error: required command '$1' not found on PATH."
    exit 1
  fi
}

for arg in "$@"; do
  case "$arg" in
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=true
      ;;
    --help|-h)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    -*)
      echo "Error: unknown option '$arg'." >&2
      usage
      exit 1
      ;;
    *)
      if [[ -n "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
        echo "Error: too many arguments." >&2
        usage
        exit 1
      fi
      BACKUP_FILE="$arg"
      ;;
  esac
done

if [[ -z "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
  echo "Error: missing required <backup-file> argument." >&2
  usage
  exit 1
fi

require_cmd curl
require_cmd jq

if [[ ! -f "$BACKUP_FILE" ]]; then
  log "Error: backup file '$BACKUP_FILE' not found."
  exit 1
fi

if ! jq empty "$BACKUP_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  log "Error: '$BACKUP_FILE' is not valid JSON."
  exit 1
fi

if [[ "$(jq -r 'type' "$BACKUP_FILE")" != "array" ]]; then
  log "Error: '$BACKUP_FILE' does not contain a JSON array of cases."
  exit 1
fi

case_count="$(jq 'length' "$BACKUP_FILE")"

if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == true ]]; then
  log "Dry run: $case_count case(s) found in $BACKUP_FILE"
else
  log "Restoring $case_count case(s) from $BACKUP_FILE"
fi

restored=0
while IFS= read -r case_json; do
  case_id="$(jq -r '.case_id // empty' <<<"$case_json")"
  if [[ -z "$case_id" ]]; then
    log "Error: found a case in the backup with no case_id. Aborting."
    exit 1
  fi

  if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == true ]]; then
    log "[DRY RUN] would PUT $BASE_URL/cases/$case_id"
    restored=$((restored + 1))
    continue
  fi

  response="$(curl -sS -X PUT -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    --data-binary "$case_json" -w $'\n%{http_code}' "$BASE_URL/cases/$case_id" || true)"
  http_code="${response##*$'\n'}"
  body="${response%$'\n'*}"

  if [[ "$http_code" != "200" ]]; then
    log "Error: PUT $BASE_URL/cases/$case_id failed (HTTP ${http_code:-no response}): $body"
    exit 1
  fi

  log "Restored $case_id"
  restored=$((restored + 1))
done < <(jq -c '.[]' "$BACKUP_FILE")

if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == true ]]; then
  log "Dry run complete: $restored case(s) would be restored"
else
  log "Restore complete: $restored case(s) restored from $BACKUP_FILE"
fi

201	
202	## Operations
203	
204	Running via Docker, plus backup/restore tooling under `ops/`. None of this
205	needs the JVM or Gradle on the host — only Docker.
206	
207	### Running via Docker
208	
209	```bash
210	docker compose up -d --build
211	```
212	
213	Builds the image (multi-stage: JDK to compile, JRE to run, non-root user)
214	and starts the backend on `http://localhost:8080` — same app as
215	`./gradlew bootRun`, just containerized. `docker-compose.yml` sets
216	`restart: "no"` on purpose: `CaseStore` is in-memory, so an auto-restarting
217	container would come back up with an empty store, silently losing whatever
218	was there before the crash.
219	
220	### `ops/run.sh` and the Makefile
221	
222	`ops/run.sh` wraps Docker Compose and Gradle with the usual lifecycle
223	subcommands; the `Makefile` just calls it so you can use whichever you
224	prefer:
225	
226	```bash
227	./ops/run.sh build   /  make build    # docker compose build
228	./ops/run.sh start   /  make start    # docker compose up -d
229	./ops/run.sh stop    /  make stop     # docker compose down
230	./ops/run.sh test    /  make test     # ./gradlew test (doesn't need Docker)
231	./ops/run.sh logs    /  make logs     # follow container logs
232	./ops/run.sh clean   /  make clean    # stop containers, remove the image, ./gradlew clean
233	./ops/run.sh --help  /  make help
234	```
235	
236	Every subcommand except `test` and `--help` checks `docker info` first and
237	fails with a one-line error if Docker isn't running, instead of letting a
238	raw Docker error leak through.
239	
240	### Backups and restores
241	
242	`backup.sh` fetches every case from a running backend and writes them to a
243	single timestamped, gitignored file:
244	
245	```bash
246	make backup
247	# [2026-06-21T11:51:11Z] Starting backup from http://localhost:8080/cases
248	# [2026-06-21T11:51:11Z] Backup complete: 1 case(s) written to backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json
249	```
250	
251	`restore.sh` takes a backup file and puts each case back exactly as it was,
252	via `PUT /cases/{caseId}` — not the follow-up endpoint, so merge logic never
253	runs. Use `--dry-run` to see what it would do first:
254	
255	```bash
256	make restore FILE=backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json DRY_RUN=1
257	make restore FILE=backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json
258	```
259	
260	Restoring is idempotent: `PUT` always sets the case to exactly the state in
261	the file, so restoring the same file twice leaves the case unchanged rather
262	than compounding anything.
263	
264	### Why `PUT /cases/{caseId}` exists separately from `POST /follow-ups`
265	
266	These two write paths look similar but do very different things:
267	
268	| Endpoint | Purpose | Runs merge logic? |
269	|---|---|---|
270	| `POST /cases/{caseId}/follow-ups` | Normal application flow — apply a new follow-up report to a case | Yes — computes `NEW`/`OVERRIDDEN`/`UNCHANGED`/`RETAINED` per field |
271	| `PUT /cases/{caseId}` | **Ops only**, used by `restore.sh` to put a case back exactly as a backup recorded it | No — raw overwrite, verbatim |
272	
273	If `restore.sh` used the follow-up endpoint instead, the restored snapshot
274	would get merged against whatever the live case currently looks like, which
275	could change field statuses and values instead of recreating the backed-up
276	state exactly. `PUT` exists so a restore is a true rollback, not another
277	merge — it isn't meant to be used as a general "update a case" endpoint.
278	
279	`GET /cases` (list all cases) exists for the same reason: `backup.sh` needs
280	to discover every case id without already knowing them upfront.
281

file.content:
## Operations

Running via Docker, plus backup/restore tooling under `ops/`. None of this
needs the JVM or Gradle on the host — only Docker.

### Running via Docker

```bash
docker compose up -d --build
```

Builds the image (multi-stage: JDK to compile, JRE to run, non-root user)
and starts the backend on `http://localhost:8080` — same app as
`./gradlew bootRun`, just containerized. `docker-compose.yml` sets
`restart: "no"` on purpose: `CaseStore` is in-memory, so an auto-restarting
container would come back up with an empty store, silently losing whatever
was there before the crash.

### `ops/run.sh` and the Makefile

`ops/run.sh` wraps Docker Compose and Gradle with the usual lifecycle
subcommands; the `Makefile` just calls it so you can use whichever you
prefer:

```bash
./ops/run.sh build   /  make build    # docker compose build
./ops/run.sh start   /  make start    # docker compose up -d
./ops/run.sh stop    /  make stop     # docker compose down
./ops/run.sh test    /  make test     # ./gradlew test (doesn't need Docker)
./ops/run.sh logs    /  make logs     # follow container logs
./ops/run.sh clean   /  make clean    # stop containers, remove the image, ./gradlew clean
./ops/run.sh --help  /  make help
```

Every subcommand except `test` and `--help` checks `docker info` first and
fails with a one-line error if Docker isn't running, instead of letting a
raw Docker error leak through.

### Backups and restores

`backup.sh` fetches every case from a running backend and writes them to a
single timestamped, gitignored file:

```bash
make backup
# [2026-06-21T11:51:11Z] Starting backup from http://localhost:8080/cases
# [2026-06-21T11:51:11Z] Backup complete: 1 case(s) written to backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json
```

`restore.sh` takes a backup file and puts each case back exactly as it was,
via `PUT /cases/{caseId}` — not the follow-up endpoint, so merge logic never
runs. Use `--dry-run` to see what it would do first:

```bash
make restore FILE=backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json DRY_RUN=1
make restore FILE=backups/cases-20260621T115111Z-LfgkHWYu.json
```

Restoring is idempotent: `PUT` always sets the case to exactly the state in
the file, so restoring the same file twice leaves the case unchanged rather
than compounding anything.

### Why `PUT /cases/{caseId}` exists separately from `POST /follow-ups`

These two write paths look similar but do very different things:

| Endpoint | Purpose | Runs merge logic? |
|---|---|---|
| `POST /cases/{caseId}/follow-ups` | Normal application flow — apply a new follow-up report to a case | Yes — computes `NEW`/`OVERRIDDEN`/`UNCHANGED`/`RETAINED` per field |
| `PUT /cases/{caseId}` | **Ops only**, used by `restore.sh` to put a case back exactly as a backup recorded it | No — raw overwrite, verbatim |

If `restore.sh` used the follow-up endpoint instead, the restored snapshot
would get merged against whatever the live case currently looks like, which
could change field statuses and values instead of recreating the backed-up
state exactly. `PUT` exists so a restore is a true rollback, not another
merge — it isn't meant to be used as a general "update a case" endpoint.

`GET /cases` (list all cases) exists for the same reason: `backup.sh` needs
to discover every case id without already knowing them upfront.

needs the JVM or Gradle on the host — only Docker.

`Makefile`, `Dockerfile`, `docker-compose.yml`, and `ops/` all live at the
